interaction_boundary: Visitors inspect cached ranks, probabilities, timing, traces, and an already-recorded intervention. No model inference, upload, recording, or live steering occurs in this bundle.

asr.label: Whisper ASR
asr.description: Three rights-cleared LibriSpeech reports showing late emergence, mixed confidence, subword structure, and a model-error path.

provenance.extraction: Replayed locally through the pinned model and fitted artifact; semantic realized-token ranks were recomputed over the full vocabulary because the browser API serializes only bounded lens candidates.

rank_semantics.tracked_target_policy: Generated target tokens whose decoded text contains at least one Unicode alphanumeric character.

timing_semantics.warning: Token times are Whisper-derived and are not independent alignment evidence.

rights.attribution: LibriSpeech dev-clean, prepared by Vassil Panayotov, Guoguo Chen, Daniel Povey, and Sanjeev Khudanpur; derived from public-domain LibriVox recordings.
rights.modification_notice: The three published input FLAC files are copied without modification from the repository's bundled examples.

examples.title: A short question: late lexical emergence
examples.teaching_purpose: Show that realized words need not be readable at early decoder layers: “now” moves from #6,319 to #7,237 before reaching #3 at L2.

fit_relationship.detail: The Whisper lens was fit on a separate ten-clip local TTS corpus; this LibriSpeech input is disjoint.

tracks.note: Late emergence: the opening token enters the top 15 only at L2.
Selected contrast: rank worsens at L1, then jumps to #3 at L2.

caveats: The actual-head probabilities are raw teacher-forced probabilities before Whisper generation suppression and timestamp rules.
Rank is not calibrated confidence, and a fitted readout is not a causal intervention.
Token timing is Whisper-derived cross-attention DTW, not independent human alignment.

examples.title: Sir, I exist: content resolves at different depths
examples.teaching_purpose: Contrast an input-related content token that reaches #17 only at L2 with “Sir,” whose realized path is merely #2 at the actual output head.

input.transcript: A man said to the universe, sir, I exist.

fit_relationship.detail: The Whisper lens was fit on a separate ten-clip local TTS corpus; this LibriSpeech input is disjoint.

model_output.text: A man said to the universe, Sir, I exist.
model_output.difference_from_reference: Whisper capitalizes “Sir”; the model path is preserved.

tracks.note: The content word becomes readable late, reaching #17 only at L2.
The realized token is only #2 at the actual head, an honest low-confidence output decision.
Strongly non-monotonic fitted trajectory that resolves only near the output.

caveats: The selected examples are illustrative trajectories, not population-level evidence.
The actual head emits “Sir” on a path where it is rank #2; realized does not always mean top-1 under the raw teacher-forced head.
Token timing is model-derived and bidirectional encoder results are intentionally omitted from this positive decoder report.

examples.title: The buzzer’s whirr: follow the model’s wrong path
examples.teaching_purpose: Expose a transcription error rather than correcting it: the fitted lens tracks generated “buzz” + “ers,” while the model omits and substitutes words from the reference.

input.transcript: The buzzer's whirr triggered his muscles into complete relaxation.

fit_relationship.detail: The Whisper lens was fit on a separate ten-clip local TTS corpus; this LibriSpeech input is disjoint.

model_output.text: The buzzers were triggered as muscles into complete relaxation.
model_output.difference_from_reference: The generated path changes “buzzer's whirr” to “buzzers were” and drops “his”; these errors are retained.

tracks.note: The realized error-path stem remains poorly ranked even at L2.
The suffix becomes readable much earlier than the stem.
A high-confidence-looking final emission can have a weak fitted trajectory.
Late fitted-readout failure despite confident actual-head emission.
The fitted lens remains wrong even though the actual head is nearly certain.

caveats: A J-lens explains the model-generated path; it is not an oracle for the reference transcript.
The “buzz” stem and “ers” suffix occupy separate BPE positions and their ranks must not be added into a word confidence.
Very confident actual-head emissions can coexist with poor fitted ranks, which can indicate a lens limitation rather than absent model information.

speech.label: LFM speech-to-speech
speech.description: One explicitly in-sample integration run and two held-out runs from a provisional one-clip projected language lens; generated speech is not redistributed.

rank_semantics.eligible_token_policy: Decoded token is nonempty, contains no angle-bracket control marker or replacement character, and contains at least one Unicode alphanumeric character.
rank_semantics.tracked_target_policy: Realized generated text positions excluding LFM text controls 7 (IM_END), 128 (AUDIO_START), and 130 (TEXT_END), plus tokenizer-declared special IDs.
rank_semantics.aggregate_policy: Median rank, mean reciprocal rank, and threshold counts are computed over every tracked target in the example, not only favorable tokens.

rights_policy.included_audio: Only the three source-input references are cleared for a future static export under LibriSpeech CC BY 4.0.
rights_policy.excluded_audio: Generated LFM speech and local macOS-TTS screening clips are intentionally absent pending model-output and voice-output redistribution review.
rights_policy.attribution: LibriSpeech dev-clean, prepared by Vassil Panayotov, Guoguo Chen, Daniel Povey, and Sanjeev Khudanpur; derived from public-domain LibriVox recordings; CC BY 4.0.

examples.teaching_purpose: Show a strong late-layer trajectory and one mid-layer lexical emergence, while explicitly demonstrating why an input used to fit a one-example lens is not held-out evidence.

fit_relationship.detail: The lens was fitted on this input using only the first six generated target positions under an 18-step generation budget; later positions in this longer replay were not fit targets.

generated.text: I'm sorry, but I can't help with that. Is there something else you need assistance with?

tracked_tokens.note: Large, mostly monotonic rise into the late layers.
Readable only at L12 despite a comparatively less poor early rank.
Only ordinary target in this example to enter the top 100 by L8.
Clear late emergence without reaching lens rank one.
Honest failure: the fitted readout remains rank 46 at L14 while the actual head emits it greedily.

caveats: This is the lens's fitting input and must never be presented as held-out validation.
The lens is a one-example rank-512/2048 projection; fine ordering may change with projection rank, seed, or a multi-example fit.
Generated speech is omitted and the language lens does not explain acoustic codes or waveform samples.
examples.title: In-sample integration: late language emergence

tracks.note: Large, mostly monotonic rise into the late layers.
Readable only at L12 despite a comparatively less poor early rank.
Only ordinary target in this example to enter the top 100 by L8.
Clear late emergence without reaching lens rank one.
Honest failure: the fitted readout remains rank 46 at L14 while the actual head emits it greedily.

examples.teaching_purpose: Show held-out late-layer improvement together with tokens that remain poorly ranked or become worse, preventing a cherry-picked monotonic story.

input.transcript: A man said to the universe, sir, I exist.

fit_relationship.detail: This input was not used to fit the one-example projected lens.

generated.text: That's a profound question. It suggests that the universe is vast and complex, and that there's a lot to explore and understand about it. It's a reminder of the mysteries that still lie beyond our current knowledge.

tracked_tokens.note: Strong late-layer success from nearly the bottom of the lexical ranking.
Input-related content becomes readable late rather than early.
Nonmonotonic failure: improves sharply at L12, then worsens at L14.
Honest late-layer failure despite actual greedy emission.
Highly nonmonotonic trajectory that ends at lens rank one.

caveats: This is held out from fitting but is still only one example under one projection seed.
The long 26.32-second generated waveform is omitted from this rights-cleared evidence file.
Low actual-head probability and poor lens rank answer different questions; neither is calibrated confidence.
examples.title: Held-out speech: mixed success and non-monotonicity

tracks.note: Strong late-layer success from nearly the bottom of the lexical ranking.
Input-related content becomes readable late rather than early.
Nonmonotonic failure: improves sharply at L12, then worsens at L14.
Honest late-layer failure despite actual greedy emission.
Highly nonmonotonic trajectory that ends at lens rank one.

examples.teaching_purpose: Show that a high safety cap is not a guarantee of natural completion, and that some realized content remains poorly represented even in the latest fitted source layer.

input.transcript: The buzzer's whirr triggered his muscles into complete relaxation.

fit_relationship.detail: This input was not used to fit the one-example projected lens.

generated.text: The buzz of the swirir triggered his muscles into complete relaxation.

tracked_tokens.note: Remains unreadable through L12, then jumps to rank one at L14.
Nonmonotonic trajectory: improves by L8, collapses at L12, and recovers at L14.
Persistent fitted-readout failure at every inspected layer.
Sharp late emergence with a small L12-to-L14 reversal.
Content token becomes readable only at L12.

caveats: The model never emitted audio EOS and stopped only at the 512-step hard cap.
The 39.76-second decoded waveform is omitted and this example should never autoplay or be the default showcase.
The generated text's 'swirir' differs from the source transcript's 'whirr'; the mismatch is retained rather than corrected.
examples.title: Held-out speech: generation and fitted-lens failure

tracks.note: Remains unreadable through L12, then jumps to rank one at L14.
Nonmonotonic trajectory: improves by L8, collapses at L12, and recovers at L14.
Persistent fitted-readout failure at every inspected layer.
Sharp late emergence with a small L12-to-L14 reversal.
Content token becomes readable only at L12.

tts.label: Chatterbox TTS
tts.description: Three cached fitted-readout trajectories, including a recorded residual intervention; no generated WAV is shipped pending rights review.

rank_semantics.acoustic_code_warning: Ordinary acoustic-code IDs are learned contextual symbols, not words or published phonemes.

rights_policy.prompts: Project-authored text prompts may be displayed.
rights_policy.audio: Generated baseline and counterfactual WAV files are excluded from this preview pending conversion, S3-tokenizer, and derived-output redistribution review.

examples.title: A bridge at S9: emergence, sensitivity, and causal steering
examples.teaching_purpose: Keep three questions separate: when realized code ID 4106 becomes readable, which prompt tokens local diagnostics emphasize, and what a replayed residual edit causes.

input.prompt: A bright red train crossed the narrow bridge.

tracks.label: ID 4106 · realized acoustic code
tracks.probability_reporting: Percent values are the exact rounded values retained in the curated source.

intervention.branch_check: The residual branch exactly matched the direct forced-code branch for this run.

caveats: An acoustic-code ID has no word or phoneme label; its decoded effect depends on context and voice conditioning.
Gradient share and attention share are diagnostics, not percent causation or validated word-to-waveform alignment.
Only the replayed residual branch is causal, and it establishes this run-specific effect rather than a robust natural concept.
Generated WAVs are intentionally absent pending the recorded rights review.

examples.title: Tiny turtles: a nearly monotonic trajectory
examples.teaching_purpose: Give visitors a clean fitted-rank trajectory while disclosing that it is unusually tidy relative to the screened population.

input.prompt: Tiny turtles travel together toward the tide.

caveats: The source curation retained the exact rank trajectory but not the position or code ID; this static report does not invent either value.
This strong row is an illustration, not a claim that every acoustic code improves monotonically.
Generated audio is excluded pending the recorded derived-output review.

examples.title: Music fades: readability can reverse
examples.teaching_purpose: Counter the impression that fitted readouts must improve layer by layer: the realized code worsens at L4 and again at L12 before resolving late.

input.prompt: Music fades as the evening grows quiet.

caveats: The source curation retained the exact rank trajectory but not the position or code ID; this static report does not invent either value.
Intermediate fitted readouts are measurements, not a guaranteed refinement pipeline.
Generated audio is excluded pending the recorded derived-output review.
